rag_rules: Place assume/guarantee declarations in component type scopes for interface behavior; use implementation scopes for assign bindings.
Use `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks to contain AGREE contract elements.
generic_rules: Use visible variables and feature names in AGREE expressions; avoid AADL property names as AGREE variables.
Declare constants and equations in component type annexes when describing interface behavior.
Place implementation-level assignments in component implementation annexes after subcomponents and connections.
Formulate assumptions and guarantees using concrete bounds or named relations from the case.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE syntax includes `assume "label": expression;`, `guarantee "label": expression;`, `eq name : type = expression;`, and `const name : type = value;`.
AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
anti_patterns: Do not place component implementation bindings in component type scopes.
Do not use AADL timing properties like Deadline or Period directly as AGREE variables.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property associations and their automatic scope in AGREE
Model fusion and architecture redesign during repair
